title: UK GDPR Compliance
description: Processing personal data of UK residents requires compliance with the UK GDPR (the retained version of EU GDPR under the Data Protection Act 2018). UK GDPR is substantively similar to EU GDPR but is a separate legal instrument enforced by the Information Commissioner's Office (ICO).
trigger: Processing personal data of UK residents

title: ICO Registration
description: Most organisations that process personal data for commercial purposes must pay a data protection fee to the Information Commissioner's Office (ICO). Failure to register is a criminal offence. Annual registration fees range from £40 to £2,900 depending on company size.
trigger: Processing personal data for commercial purposes in the UK

source.citation: Data Protection Act 2018, Part 2; Data Protection (Charges and Information) Regulations 2018

title: IR35 Contractor Classification Assessment
description: Organisations engaging contractors who work primarily for one client must assess whether the engagement falls inside IR35 (off-payroll working rules). Medium and large companies in the private sector are responsible for the IR35 determination. Incorrect assessments create significant PAYE and NIC liability.
trigger: Engaging contractors who work primarily for one client

source.citation: Income Tax (Earnings and Pensions) Act 2003, Chapter 8; Finance Act 2021

title: PAYE and National Insurance Registration
description: UK employers must register as a PAYE employer with HMRC before making the first payroll payment. Employers must withhold income tax and employee National Insurance Contributions (NICs) and make employer NICs of 13.8% above the secondary threshold.

source.citation: Income Tax (Earnings and Pensions) Act 2003; Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992
